title: Template Engines in Express
content:
### EJS Setup EJS is a popular template engine for Express: ```bash npm install ejs ``` ```javascript app.set('view engine', 'ejs'); app.set('views', './views'); ``` Create `views/index.ejs`: ```html

<%= title %>

Welcome, <%= user.name %>

``` Render in route: ```javascript app.get('/', (req, res) => { res.render('index', { title: 'Home', user: { name: 'John' } }); }); ``` ### Passing Data to Templates Use `<%= %>` for escaped output and `<%- %>` for raw HTML: ```ejs <%= user.name %> <%- user.bioHtml %> <% if (user.loggedIn) { %> Logout <% } %> ``` ### Partials and Layouts (EJS) Create reusable partials: ```ejs

<%= title %>

<%- include('partials/header') %> <%- include('partials/footer') %> ``` ### Pug ```bash npm install pug ``` ```javascript app.set('view engine', 'pug'); ``` Pug uses indentation-based syntax: ```pug html head title= title body h1= message ```

quiz.question: Which method sets the template engine in Express?
What is the EJS syntax for escaped output?
